title: luzula multiflora subsp. nigricans
intro: Luzula multiflora subsp. nigricans, also known as the black woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is native to certain parts of Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and may also have a positive effect on the digestive system and respiratory health. Luzula multiflora subsp. nigricans can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for its health benefits.
